description: Represents a complex number on the complex plane with optional vector display
utility: Visualizes complex numbers in Cartesian or polar form with arithmetic operations support

arguments.name: graph
arguments.description: The 2D graph (complex plane) to plot on

arguments.name: a (or z)
arguments.description: Real part in Cartesian form, or magnitude in polar form, or a complex number to copy

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: Imaginary part in Cartesian form, or angle (degrees) in polar form. Required unless first arg is complex

alternates.description: Plot complex number a + bi as a point on the plane

alternates.syntax: complex(graph, a, b, type(2)) or complex(graph, a, b, type(vector))
alternates.description: Plot a + bi showing both point and vector from origin
Plot magnitude r at angle θ (degrees). Always shows point and vector
Create new complex number from existing complex number z

examples.scenario: Plot in polar form: magnitude 5 at 45 degrees
examples.expression: G = g2d(-5, 5, -5, 5); z = complex(G, 5, 45, type(polar), c(red))
z1 = complex(G, 1, 2); z2 = complex(G, 3, 4); sum = z1 + z2; product = z1 * z2
